summary.intent: Build a live Expo web app + local Express/WebSocket API server (claude-hook-handler serve) to display active Claude sessions and their events from a SQLite database in real time.
summary.outcome: The full stack was implemented and running: backend API server with HTTP + WebSocket on port 3000, Expo web app on port 19006, plan→implementation session linking, collapsed Pre/PostToolUse rows, and keyword chips/mailto user links — though the scrolling bug was still being actively debugged at session end.

repo: NodeNext module resolution requires .js extensions on all local imports in src/server.ts
Two separate tsconfigs must coexist: root (NodeNext) and app/ (expo/tsconfig.base) — they must not merge
Claude Code fires no UserPromptSubmit when a plan-mode session ends and the implementation session begins; the new session jumps straight to PreToolUse with no hook capturing the approval message

Plan→implementation session continuity can be detected by querying for a SessionEnd in the last ~30s with the same cwd before inserting a new SessionStart, using the same parentSessionId mechanism already used for subagents
Dynamic import of server.ts in the serve subcommand prevents express/ws from loading on every normal hook invocation
WebSocketServer can share port 3000 with Express via { server: httpServer } — no separate WS port needed
DB change detection is done by polling db.event.findFirst({ orderBy: { id: 'desc' } }) every 2s and broadcasting when the max id changes

code.finding: @react-navigation/stack renders screens in absolute-positioned divs, so flex:1 resolves to zero height and ScrollView/FlatList never clip. Fix: useWindowDimensions().height - useHeaderHeight() gives exact content-area pixel height; set it explicitly with overflow:'scroll'

FlatList nested inside a horizontal ScrollView fights for scroll ownership on web. Replaced with plain map() inside nested ScrollViews (outer vertical for pull-to-refresh, inner horizontal for table width), with the header row rendered outside the vertical scroll so it stays sticky

Pre/PostToolUse events are grouped by tool_use_id in a two-pass algorithm in SessionDetail: first build a map of tool_use_id → PostToolUse, then on second pass replace each PreToolUse with a merged item and skip the matched PostToolUse
react@18.3.2 does not exist on npm; Expo 52 requires exactly 18.3.1. Also requires @expo/metro-runtime for web support (not listed as a direct dep by Expo but needed at runtime)

workflow: npm install in an app/ subdirectory of a Node repo requires --legacy-peer-deps when the parent has a hoisted react that npm treats as version undefined
Expo 52 expected react-native version is 0.76.9 (not 0.76.3 as originally specified)
reconstruct subcommand can be re-run after logic changes to backfill derived fields (parentSessionId) retroactively into existing DB rows
Testing a CLI serve command: start with a nohup/background process, curl the API, kill by PID — avoids the need for a second terminal during smoke-testing

friction: react@18.3.2 doesn't exist; caused install failure that required correcting the package.json version to 18.3.1
npm hoisting conflict: parent node_modules react was seen as 'undefined' version by nested app/ install; required --legacy-peer-deps
@expo/metro-runtime was missing and not declared as a dep, causing silent web bundle failure
Port 3000 already in use during smoke-test (lingering background process from previous test run)

Scrolling was broken across three successive fix attempts due to @react-navigation/stack's absolute-positioned screen containers defeating flex:1 height resolution — root cause took multiple iterations to identify
useHeaderHeight import path had to be verified (lives in @react-navigation/elements, a transitive dep)
open_items: Scrolling fix (useWindowDimensions + useHeaderHeight + overflow:'scroll') was applied but not confirmed working before session ended

The first user prompt of any plan-approved session is permanently unrecoverable from hooks — no UserPromptSubmit fires for the plan-approval message; sessions will always show an empty Summary until the user sends a follow-up
react-native version mismatch warning (0.76.9 vs Expo 52 expectation) may still appear after the package.json update
No authentication or rate limiting on the local API server — acceptable for a local dev tool but noted as out of scope
